features.name: Feedback loop through module instantiation
features.description: Code should include a feedback path that crosses module boundaries: a wire driven by a register output is passed to an instance input port, and the same wire is also driven by the instance's output port, creating a loop through module hierarchy.

features.name: Expression-driven instance port connection
features.description: Code should include an instance input port connected to a compound expression rather than a simple wire or port reference, where the expression references a wire also driven by the instance's output.

features.name: Registered wire driving hierarchical feedback
features.description: Code should include a register whose output wire forms part of a feedback path that crosses module boundaries, where the same wire is both the register's output and connected to an instance output port.
features.snippet:
reg out_q;
always @(posedge clk) begin if (rst) out_q <= 0; else out_q <= in_b; end
